channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration moving to fire FBI agents involved in investigations of Trump, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trump administration officials are moving to fire at least some of the FBI agents who participated in investigations involving President Donald Trump in the coming days, two people familiar with the plans said Friday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon prepares to deploy 1,000 more troops to bolster Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: U.S. officials say the Pentagon is readying orders for the deployment of at least 1,000 additional active duty troops to bolster President Donald Trump’s expanding crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Colombian president urges illegal immigrants in US to return home days after diplomatic spat
item.description: The president of Colombia is urging his nationals in the U.S. illegally to return to Colombia, days after a diplomatic spat with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A list of government web pages that have gone dark to comply with Trump orders
item.description: U.S. government websites have gone dark as agencies scrambled to comply with President Donald Trump’s executive orders declaring his administration would recognize only two genders and ordering an end to diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DOGE announces more than $1B in savings after canceling 104 federal DEI contracts
item.description: Two dozen federal agencies reported a collective $1 billion in savings from the elimination of DEIA/DEI offices and roles within their bureaucracies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump targets FAA diversity efforts in plane crash probe despite no evidence they played any role
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump wasted little time this week trying to assign blame for the nation’s deadliest air disaster in more than two decades. Among his chief targets: An FAA diversity hiring initiative he suggested had undermined the agency’s effectiveness.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, ALI SWENSON and ALEXANDRA OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legal experts say Kash Patel's opposition to warrant requirement is not a major split
item.description: Legal experts say that while FBI director nominee Kash Patel's opposition to a warrant requirement shocked some lawmakers, the warrant requirement debate is far from new.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dem bill blames LA wildfire damage on fossil fuel emissions, holds oil and gas industry liable
item.description: California Democrats are seeking to make the oil and gas industry pay for damages from the Los Angeles wildfires, shifting the blame from state policy to climate change.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois governor blocks pardoned Jan. 6 rioters from state jobs
item.description: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker (D) said he would block pardoned Capitol rioters from serving in local government positions, citing a state law ordinance, according to a Thursday letter obtained by The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senator faces intraparty criticism after saying he is 'struggling' with RFK Jr's confirmation
item.description: The top Republican on the Senate's health committee, Sen. Bill Cassidy, R-La., faced criticism from fellow Republicans after he suggested his vote for Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s confirmation as Health and Human Services secretary is not a lock.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump makes his first moves on education, but no McMahon hearing in sight
item.description: President Trump made his first major moves on education Wednesday, signing a series of executive orders that come even before his nominee to lead the Education Department, Linda McMahon, has had her Senate confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senator 'surprised' Gabbard refused to call Snowden a traitor
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.), a member of the Senate Intelligence Committee, said he was “surprised” that director of national intelligence nominee Tulsi Gabbard refused to call former NSA contractor Edward Snowden a “traitor” after being repeatedly pressed on the question at a Thursday hearing.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Black Caucus blasts Trump's DEI comments after Potomac plane crash
item.description: "Not only are the President’s claims untrue, they also speak to the Republican Party’s desire to divide us as a country.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds protest immigration policies at Trump Hotel in Vegas
item.description: Around 200 people gathered outside the Trump Hotel in Vegas to push back against the administration's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Sasha Loftis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests FAA's diversity efforts caused safety issues in crash
item.description: Trump weighed in on a crash between an American Airlines plane and a military helicopter, claiming it “should have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trans community fears Trump's actions will upend legal precedent on prison protections
item.description: One of President Trump's first executive orders impacts transgender inmates incarcerated in federal facilities. It's just one in a series of actions signed by Trump that target the trans community.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Trump order cracks down on antisemitism and could deport foreign student protesters
item.description: The executive order directs government agencies to use all available tools to prosecute or remove perpetrators of antisemitic harassment and violence, especially on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs Laken Riley Act, plans to send illegal immigrants to Guantanamo
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's directing the opening of a detention center at Guantanamo Bay to hold up to 30,000 migrants who are living without legal permission in the United States. The U.S. military base has been used to house detainees from the U.S. war on terrorism.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs Laken Riley Act, marking first legislative win of second term
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed the Laken Riley Act into law, marking his first legislative victory since he returned to the White House after he campaigned on immigration and the border during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota ACLU: Local jurisdictions have the right to not cooperate with immigration enforcement
item.description: During President Donald Trump’s first week in office, his new Department of Justice announced plans to potentially criminally prosecute local and state officials who fail to “comply with lawful immigration-related commands and requests.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Ngoc Bui, MPR News

item.title: Center for Victims of Torture dramatically slashes workforce after Trump administration orders
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive orders forced many international humanitarian organizations, like St. Paul-based Center for Victims of Torture, to cut spending and furlough staff.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News
